title: A Dementia Strategy for Canada: Together We Aspire
full_text: Canada's first national dementia strategy sets three national objectives: prevent dementia, advance therapies and find a cure, and improve the quality of life of people living with dementia and caregivers. Budget 2019 committed funding to support implementation, including a Dementia Strategic Fund and dementia awareness and surveillance investments.

title: New Horizons for Seniors Program
full_text: A federal grants and contributions program funding community-based projects that help seniors stay active, engaged, and socially connected, reducing isolation. Annual community and pan-Canadian funding streams support volunteerism, mentorship, and social participation for older adults.

title: Guaranteed Income Supplement — Top-up and Earnings Exemption
full_text: Enhancements to the Guaranteed Income Supplement increased benefits for low-income single seniors and expanded the earnings exemption, improving the financial security of the most vulnerable older adults receiving Old Age Security.

title: SHIFT: Nova Scotia's Action Plan for an Aging Population
full_text: A whole-of-government action plan to help Nova Scotians age well, framed around financial security, healthy and active living, age-friendly communities, and supporting older adults to live independently. It coordinates programs across departments to support aging in place.

title: Action for Health: Nova Scotia's Health Plan
department: NS Department of Health and Wellness
full_text: A multi-year strategic plan to improve access, quality, and sustainability of Nova Scotia's health system. Solutions include expanding primary care access (community pharmacy clinics, urgent care), improving home care and long-term care, and public reporting of measures through an open dashboard.

title: Home Care Expansion and Improvement
department: NS Department of Seniors and Long-Term Care
full_text: Investments to expand home support and home care services so more older Nova Scotians can remain in their homes longer, including increased service hours, recruitment of continuing care assistants, and self-managed care options.

title: Long-Term Care: Build, Renovate, Replace
department: NS Department of Seniors and Long-Term Care
full_text: A capital plan to add new long-term care beds and replace or renovate existing facilities, prioritizing single rooms to improve resident quality of life and infection control, alongside increased direct-care hours per resident day.

title: Continuing Care Assistant Workforce Strategy
department: NS Department of Seniors and Long-Term Care
full_text: A workforce strategy to recruit and retain Continuing Care Assistants through free tuition bursaries, wage increases, and accelerated training, addressing staffing shortages in home care and long-term care.
